title: "WSL version should be >= 1.2.5" when installing Podman via Podman Desktop, but fully up to date
body:
### Issue Description

Hello

So I just tried installing the latest upstream release directly from this bug report which i did not find anywhere and that worked... I'm leaving this as a bug report because i lost a full day trying to make this work and it would be nice is Podman Desktop was fixed.

--

I am installing Podman from Podman Desktop for the first time, after coming from Rancher Desktop (Not supported by DotNet Aspire) and before that Docker Desktop (Licencing issues).
I have fully uninstalled everything now and started from fresh. didn't wsl --install, installed via the store, wsl --update, made sure that i have a sufficent update for winows.
Ubuntu in WSL2 works fine. When i run wsl -l -v i have Ubuntu and 2.
What I do not have is a wsl --version. It says the argument doesn't exist, except in the installer for Podman is Podman Desktop the message is 

"WSL version should be >= 1.2.5.
Call 'wsl --update' and 'wsl --version' in a terminal to check your wsl version."

I have a wsl --status that gives me 

"Default Distribution: Ubuntu
Default Version: 2

Windows Subsystem for Linux was last updated on 15/08/2025
WSL automatic updates are on.

Kernel version: 5.10.102.1"

Does anyone have any idea what I'm doing wrong or is it a bug with the installer ?

Thanking you in advance !


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman Desktop
2. Follow the instruction to install podman
  -> on the step to verify system requirement the problem appears


### Describe the results you received

Above

### Describe the results you expected

That it accept my current version as it is completely up to date and functional

### podman info output

```yaml
podman is not yet installed and not available via the command line
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
I would suggest running `wsl --uninstall` in PowerShell, and then running `wsl --install`. I'm not sure what is included when you install Ubuntu from the Microsoft Store.

After that, you should be able to run [`wsl --version`](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/basic-commands#check-wsl-version).

I wasn't able to replicate the issue you're describing. What version of Windows are you running?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

~~I am transferring this issue to the Podman Desktop as~~ it looks like a Podman Desktop installer issue (the Podman installer doesn't check for the WSL version). The core issue appears to be that the WSL CLI is an outdated version, which is surprising since you've already run the command `wsl --update`. Please provide the version of Windows, as requested by @Honny1. This would help us understand what's going on.
We cannot transfer issues to Podman Desktop, but maybe @benoitf has an idea?
I had a similar issue, and the step I was lacking was installing Podman with the setup file (not the installer).
Let's close it, as we don't have enough information (e.g., the Windows version) to reproduce the problem. We will reopen it if we get more details.
